fields.description:
## Why

A cluster of low-severity hygiene items from the 2026-06-18 audit: a confirmed dead
parallel implementation (~207 LOC), a latent boolean trap, an inconsistent HTTP-status
access pattern, a missing `__all__` entry, and two stale internal annotations. Grouped
into one low-risk pass.

## What

Delete confirmed dead code; make `reduce_all_tickets`'s booleans keyword-only;
consolidate the inconsistent HTTP-status access in the reconciler; add `attach_commits`
to `rebar.__all__`; and annotate two previously-flagged (benign) sites.

## Scope

- `_engine_support/bridge_fsck.py` (delete dead `enumerate_*`).
- `reducer/_api.py` (`reduce_all_tickets` keyword-only).
- `_engine/rebar_reconciler/_errors.py`, `apply_outbound.py`, `batch_dispatch.py` (HTTP status).
- `__init__.py` (`attach_commits` in `__all__`).
- `config.py`, `_engine_support/resolver.py` (annotations).

## Delivery notes (deviations recorded honestly)

- **HTTP status:** consolidated the genuine cross-convention inconsistency (the
  `JiraAPIError` sites + the ad-hoc `getattr(status_code) or getattr(code)` normalizer)
  via `_errors.http_status`/`is_not_found`. Left `outbound_differ.py:107` as-is — it
  catches `urllib.HTTPError` specifically (so `.code` is correct) and deliberately has
  no top-level reconciler imports. A broader "name every HTTP literal everywhere" sweep
  is left as an optional future cleanup.
- **`__all__` sort:** added `attach_commits`, but did NOT blanket-sort the `__all__`
  lists — `rebar.__all__` is intentionally grouped with section comments and RUF022 is
  not in the project's ruff gate, so a forced alphabetical sort would harm curation for
  no gated benefit.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [x] The four `bridge_fsck.enumerate_*` functions are deleted (~207 LOC);
  `audit_bridge_mappings` and the bridge tests still pass; no dangling references.
- [x] `reduce_all_tickets`'s three booleans are keyword-only (`*` added); full reducer
  suite green.
- [x] Reconciler HTTP-status checks at the inconsistent `JiraAPIError`/normalizer sites
  use the shared `http_status`/`is_not_found` helpers (one consistent access form);
  reconciler apply/orchestrate suites pass.
- [x] `attach_commits` is in `rebar.__all__` (blanket RUF022 sort intentionally skipped
  — see delivery notes).
- [x] `config._CLI_OVERRIDES` carries a "CLI-only, not set under MCP" note;
  `resolver.py`'s function-local `_alias` import carries the circular-dep note.
- [x] ruff clean on all changed files; affected suites green.
</content>
